mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned GLC43 engine typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only depending on generation (M276 V6 or M139l four‑cylinder). Reconditioned engines typically cost £3,500–£5,500, with total installed cost around £4,800–£7,000. Fitting adds £1,300–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation your GLC43 has. The M276 V6 (2016‑2023) is a smooth, characterful engine but has known weaknesses – fuel injector failure (reported at as little as 4,000 miles) and oil leaks from camshaft adjuster solenoids. The M139l four‑cylinder (2023‑present) is a technical masterpiece – the most powerful production four‑cylinder in the world – but it is a highly stressed engine that demands rigorous maintenance. Both engines are handcrafted and require regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles).

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑AMG GLC43?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are fuel injector failure on the M276 (causing rough idle, misfire, hard starting), oil leaks from the camshaft adjuster solenoids on the M276, a safety recall for the integral carrier on 2024 models, high‑stress engine issues on the M139l, and AMG SPEEDSHIFT MCT 9‑speed transmission problems – described as 'wonky' with rough shifts, hesitating, and jerky gear changes.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a GLC43 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £20,000–£50,000 GLC43, a £3,500–£6,500 recon is 10‑35% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the engine has suffered catastrophic failure (cylinder scoring, snapped timing chain), a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my GLC43's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X253/C253 (2016‑2023) GLC43 uses the M276.823 – a 3.0‑litre V6 biturbo. The X254/C254 (2023‑present) GLC43 uses the M139l – a 2.0‑litre four‑cylinder turbo with 48V mild‑hybrid. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a GLC43 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – M276 fuel injector failure, oil leaks, M139l high‑stress engine issues – are repairable. However, if the engine has suffered catastrophic failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild. The 2024 integral carrier recall is a safety‑critical issue that must be addressed by a dealer.

eraMap.title: Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 390 PS, 520 Nm — smooth V6, fuel injector and timing chain issues are the main concerns
421 PS, 500 Nm — F1‑derived electric turbo, 48V mild‑hybrid, highly stressed four‑cylinder

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page must match. Sections 2, 4, and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the GLC43 AMG pricing across generations. Used M276.823 engines are available through specialist suppliers. Reconditioned engines typically cost £3,500–£5,500, with total installed cost around £4,800–£7,000. The M139l is at the upper end of the range due to its newer technology and limited supply. Labour costs are higher than for conventional engines due to the complex packaging of the GLC's engine bay.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a GLC43 engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent Mercedes specialist). The M139l's electric turbocharger and 48V hybrid system add significant labour complexity.

cta.label: → Compare GLC43 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: M276 Fuel Injector Failure (Pre‑facelift, 2016–2023)
problems.symptoms: Long cranking on start (hot or cold), loss of power, engine stalling, engine warning light, rough idle, misfires. Some owners have reported fuel injector issues at as little as 4,000 miles.
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000–60,000 miles — can occur at low mileage.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (HPFP or injector replacement — injectors may need coding).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored injector failure can damage catalytic converters).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if hard starting or rough running occurs.

problems.recommendation: The M276 V6's fuel injectors are a known weak point. Owners have reported that "there is misfiring and an issue with my fuel injectors" and the car has been in the shop for 28 days "waiting on parts". The high‑pressure fuel system places enormous demands on the injectors. Regular use of high‑quality premium fuel and occasional fuel system cleaner can help prevent issues. If you experience hard starting or rough running, have the fuel system inspected immediately.

problems.title: M276 Camshaft Adjuster Solenoid Oil Leaks (Pre‑facelift, 2016–2023)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top of the engine. Oil leaks from the camshaft adjuster solenoids and wiring harnesses are common.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (camshaft adjuster solenoid seal and wiring harness inspection/repair).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: The M276 is known for oil leaks from the camshaft adjuster solenoids. Oil can migrate through the wiring harness, causing sensor and control unit issues. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the camshaft adjuster solenoid seals and wiring harness inspected. Early intervention can prevent more expensive electrical failures.

problems.title: Integral Carrier Failure — Safety Recall (2024 Models)
problems.symptoms: Loss of vehicle control, unusual noises from the front of the vehicle, steering issues. The integral carrier that supports the engine may fail due to improper welds.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affects certain 2024 GLC 300 and AMG GLC 43 vehicles.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice unusual noises or steering issues, stop driving immediately. A loss of vehicle control is a safety‑critical issue.

problems.recommendation: Mercedes‑Benz has issued a safety recall for certain 2024 GLC 300 and AMG GLC 43 vehicles. The integral carrier that supports the engine may fail due to improper welds, which can cause a loss of vehicle control. Check your VIN against the recall database immediately. If you notice any unusual noises, steering issues, or loss of control, stop driving and have the vehicle recovered.

problems.title: M139l High‑Stress Engine Issues (Facelift, 2023–present)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, rough idle, misfire, loss of power, engine warning light, oil consumption.
problems.typicalMileage: 10,000–30,000 miles (the engine is still relatively new — long‑term reliability data is emerging).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms develop.

problems.recommendation: The M139l is the most powerful series‑production four‑cylinder engine in the world. It produces 421 PS from just 2.0 litres — a specific output of over 210 bhp per litre. This extreme state of tune places enormous demands on the fuel system, turbocharger, and cooling system. While the engine is a technical masterpiece, it is a high‑stress engine that demands rigorous maintenance. Regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles) using the correct MB‑spec oil are essential. The engine features an F1‑derived electric exhaust gas turbocharger — a complex component that requires specialist attention if it fails. Owners should also be aware of potential cylinder scoring issues on hard‑driven examples.

problems.title: AMG SPEEDSHIFT MCT 9‑Speed Transmission Issues (Both Generations)
problems.symptoms: Rough shifting, hesitating, jerky gear changes (particularly 1st to 2nd, 2nd to 3rd), transmission getting confused, clunking noises when shifting into reverse or drive.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (software update, valve body repair, or transmission overhaul). Owners have reported that "there's a noticeable jerk when shifting".

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms develop. If the transmission is "jerking to a stop like it was about to stall and shut the engine off", book an urgent inspection.

problems.recommendation: The AMG SPEEDSHIFT MCT 9‑speed transmission has been described as "wonky" by some reviewers. Owners have reported that "the transmission has been rough since day one especially when it's shifting from 2nd to 3rd gear" and that it "has also gotten confused on several occasions: hesitating for long periods and not knowing which gear to choose". There is a known issue in the 43 models with the MCT, and AMG is supposedly working on a software fix. If you experience jerky shifts or hesitation, have the transmission control module (TCM) software checked — a software update may resolve the issue. In more severe cases, valve body repair or clutch replacement may be required.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth fixing — common on high‑output engines
Recall repair is free — check your VIN
Worth fixing — common on highly stressed engines
Software update may resolve; worth fixing
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes‑AMG GLC43? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles)
* X253/C253 (M276): Injectors in good health — no hard starting or rough idle
* X253/C253 (M276): No oil leaks from the camshaft adjuster solenoids
* X254/C254 (M139l): The engine is still under warranty — repairs on the new four‑cylinder can be expensive
* A well‑maintained example — the GLC43 is a high‑performance vehicle that demands attention
avoidIf: * X253/C253 (M276): Any sign of hard starting or misfire — injector warning
* X253/C253 (M276): Any sign of oil leaks from the top of the engine
* X254/C254 (M139l): Any sign of rough running or hard starting — high‑stress engine warning
* 2024 models: No evidence of recall completion — integral carrier failure is safety‑critical
* Any sign of jerky gear changes — transmission issues are common
* Poor service history — both engines are intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 is a compelling performance SUV — offering a potent blend of luxury, practicality, and AMG‑bred performance. The pre‑facelift X253/C253 (2016‑2023) with the M276 V6 is a smooth, characterful engine that has known weaknesses — fuel injectors and oil leaks. The facelift X254/C254 (2023‑present) with the M139l four‑cylinder is a technical masterpiece — the most powerful production four‑cylinder in the world — but it is a highly stressed engine that demands rigorous maintenance. Both generations have issues with the AMG SPEEDSHIFT MCT 9‑speed transmission — described as "wonky" with rough shifts and hesitation. Buy on service history above all else — a well‑maintained GLC43 is a rewarding and exciting car; a neglected example can be expensive to repair. As one owner noted: "I got my Glc 43 amg in July. 4000 miles in they are saying that there is misfiring and an issue with my fuel injectors" — even low‑mileage cars can have issues.

cta.label: → Compare GLC43 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑AMG GLC43 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any hard starting, rough idle, or misfire? (injector warning)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Injector Health — Any history of injector issues? (M276 common issue)
4. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the top of the engine for any signs of leakage
5. Recall Status — 2024 models: Integral carrier recall completed? This is safety‑critical
6. Transmission — Any jerky shifts, hesitating, or clunking? (MCT transmission warning)
7. Running Quality — Any rough running or loss of power? (M139l warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
